chunk.name: Confirmation dialog
chunk.description: A modal that asks the user to confirm or cancel a destructive action before it runs. Composes Dialog, Stack, Text, and Button to present a clear question and two explicit choices.

relationships.role: Hosts the confirmation in a modal surface
Renders the confirm and cancel actions

documentBlocks.purpose: Ask the user to confirm or cancel a destructive action before it runs.

items.description: Guard a destructive or irreversible action — deleting, archiving, or overwriting — so the user confirms intent before it runs.
Confirm low-stakes, easily reversible actions where an undo affordance would serve the user better.

alternative.rationale: A toast with an undo action keeps reversible flows fast and avoids interrupting the user with a modal.

items.guidance: Label the confirm button with the action it performs (e.g. 'Delete') rather than a generic 'OK'.
items.rationale: An action-specific label tells the user what will happen, reducing accidental confirmations.

items.guidance: Give the destructive confirm button the 'critical' tone and keep the cancel action visually quieter.
items.rationale: Tone and weight signal the consequence and steer the user toward the safe default.

items.guidance: Move focus into the dialog on open and return it to the trigger on close.
items.rationale: Keyboard and screen reader users lose their place when focus is not managed across the modal lifecycle.

items.label: Keep the confirm and cancel actions as Button components — do not replace them with raw button elements.
Replace the placeholder question and confirm label with copy naming the specific action being confirmed.
Preserve the focus-management wiring when restructuring the dialog contents.
